atoms.semantic_claim: EthChainSpec exposes an optional per-chain maximum header extra_data size, with None meaning unbounded.

destinations.evidence: EthChainSpec now exposes the optional per-chain header extra_data cap.

destinations.snippet: max_extra_data_bytes: None for genesis-derived specs; Ethereum static specs default to Some(MAXIMUM_EXTRA_DATA_SIZE)
destinations.evidence: Genesis-derived Ritual/Summit chains can now be unbounded while standard Ethereum specs retain the 32-byte cap.
Consensus header validation now reads the per-chain cap instead of using a hard-coded maximum.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Per-chain extra_data cap behavior is restored with None meaning unbounded, and consensus consumes it.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Load a Summit genesis that requires unbounded header extra_data and verify consensus validation consults the chain spec instead of a fixed Ethereum max.
atoms.notes: Covered by chain spec and consensus focused tests in this pass.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: feature-preservation-map.md section 9: Chainspec, Genesis, And Config
